05: May the laughs be with you! Warmer weather, longer days, and comedians who've finally figured out their material.
06: Summer is coming! Laugh your way into the season before you realize you haven't achieved your New Year's resolutions.
Zürich's June sun refuses to set before 9pm, giving you absolutely no excuse not to come out and laugh.
In a city famous for running on time, comedians remain the only ones cheerfully allowed to go off-script.
The lake is sparkling, the Alps are showing off, and somewhere indoors a comedian is about to absolutely destroy a room.
07: The heat is on! Cool down with comedy that's hotter than the Swiss summer sun.
July in Zürich: the sun stays out until ten, the comedians stay on until eleven, and everyone pretends they have work tomorrow.
The city is warm, the beer gardens are full, and somewhere on a small stage someone is about to say the thing you were thinking.
Switzerland may have invented the cuckoo clock, but in July we prefer our comedians to run a little over time.
08: The lake is warm, the evenings are long, and someone is about to say something deeply unwise into a microphone.
Before the Swiss summer packs its bags and the serious season returns, come let strangers make you laugh embarrassingly hard.
Zürich in August: too hot to think, too bright to sleep, and exactly the right temperature for a bad decision at a comedy show.
09: The sweaters are out, the tourists are gone, and September in Zürich belongs entirely to people who laugh.
Precision watches, flawless trains, zero tolerance for bad cheese, and now a month of people determined to make you laugh.
September is the universe's polite warning that winter is coming, so you may as well spend it laughing.
10: The leaves are falling, the fondue is bubbling, and someone in Zürich is about to make a room full of strangers laugh until it hurts.
October in Zürich: the clocks change, the temperature drops, and the only reliable warmth left is a punchline landing exactly on time.
When October turns Zürich grey and staying out feels brave, a good comedian is all the excuse you need to leave the house.
11: November in Zürich: dark, damp, and absolutely ideal for laughing at strangers in a heated room.
The leaves are down, the fondue is bubbling, and somebody onstage is about to say something they cannot unsay.
When the fog rolls in off the lake and the days shrink to nothing, stand-up comedy starts to look like a very good idea.
12: The nights are long, the glühwein is warm, and somebody has to make the whole city laugh before the year gives up.
Forget the Christmas markets for one evening and let someone professionally ruin the carol that has been stuck in your head since November.
December in Zürich is breathtaking, borderline unaffordable, and absolutely overflowing with material for anyone holding a microphone.
